response:
<> In PHP, you can replace one URL with another using the `str_replace()` function. Here's an example of how to do it: ```php $string = "New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

"; $originalUrl = "http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1"; $newUrl = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $string = str_replace($originalUrl, $newUrl, $string); echo $string; ``` This code will replace the URL `http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1` with `http://yahoo.com/abc1` in the string. It works for repeating strings as well. A palindrome in code could be something like: ``` racecar ``` This is a simple palindrome and can be used as a test case.
